content.claim: Diagnostic authority for special education status in Poland is centralized in Psychological and Pedagogical Counselling Centres and education superintendents, not vested in school headmasters or teachers; this creates a gatekeeping structure separate from schools
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Does not quantify capacity constraints or throughput bottlenecks at Counselling and Guidance Centres
Does not address whether centralized gatekeeping reduces or merely displaces diagnostic discretion
Unclear how referral bias from schools to assessment centers affects effective supply of diagnoses
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
